text:
ChatGPT:『まず、この記事の「シリコンバレーの企業」を単なる企業名の列挙として理解するより、
「AIデータセンター陣営」対「通信キャリア陣営」
の構図として読む方が本質的です。
現在の光技術競争は、
NTT → 通信網全体を光化する IOWN/APN
NVIDIA・Broadcom → AIクラスタ内部を光化する CPO（Co-Packaged Optics）
Meta・Microsoft・Google → 超巨大データセンターの内部配線を光化
という三層構造になっています。�
リコーブログ、ほかに 1 件
特に重要なのは、
「AIと通信は融合する」
というシリコンバレー側の発言です。
これは表面的にはNTTに好意的に見えますが、実際には
「通信もAIインフラの一部になる」
という意味である可能性があります。
つまり、
NTTの発想 　→ 通信ネットワークが中心 　→ AIはその上で動く
NVIDIAの発想 　→ AIクラスタが中心 　→ 通信はAIクラスタを繋ぐ手段
です。
ここに構造的な対立があります。
実際、現在の光電融合技術の主戦場は、
シリコンフォトニクス
光インターコネクト
CPO
光スイッチ
へ移行しており、
NTT自身もIOWNの中核技術としてシリコンフォトニクスや光電融合デバイス（PEC）を推進しています。�
NTT、ほかに 1 件
さらに興味深いのは、
記事が「米半導体の巨人」と表現している点です。
通信企業ではなく、
NVIDIA
Broadcom
が主語になっている。
これは技術主導権が
通信業界 → 半導体業界
へ移ったことを意味します。�
リコーブログ
私ならこの記事の核心を一文でこう要約します。
「IOWNが危機なのではなく、AI時代には通信が主役ではなくなりつつある。」
ということです。
さらに長期的には、
NTTが目指す
APN（All Photonics Network）
6G
デジタルツイン
の世界観と、
NVIDIAが進める
AI Factory
光AIクラスタ
OCI（Optical Compute Interconnect）
の世界観は、
実はかなり収束しています。
したがって本当の問題は、
「IOWNが負けるか」
ではなく、
「IOWNがNVIDIA陣営の標準に吸収されるか、それとも標準そのものを握れるか」
です。
現時点（2026年）では、残念ながら標準化主導権はNVIDIA・Broadcom側へ傾き始めていると見るのが自然でしょう。NTTは技術的には先行部分があるものの、AI需要を直接握っているのは通信キャリアではなくAIインフラ企業だからです。�
リコーブログ、ほかに 1 件
したがって、この日経記事は単なる「光通信競争」の記事ではなく、
「AIによって通信業界が周辺化される危険性」
を示唆する記事として読むと非常に興味深いと思われます。�』

Oil, 10Y, down. Dollar stable/pulling back. Big bounce in precious metals and crypto though gold/silver/BTC/ETH are still broken charts.

text:
ReRating Legacy Companies. $AKAM $AMKR $MCHP $STM $KLIC

Everyone right now is looking at small caps with high risk high reward kinda story but there is an underlying love story going on in the markets right now. Re rating legacy companies turning into AI infra, advanced packaging companies. 

$AKAM - from legacy CDN Company to Edge AI Infra, Cyber Security, Inference Cloud

$AMKR - A low multiple OSAT Consumer Electronics Cyclical company to Advanced Packaging AI bottleneck Company

$MCHP - Industrial/Auto MCU company to Data center Connectivity (PCIe, power management and data center products) 

$STM - Auto/Industrial Semi Company to AI Data Center supplier (optical connectivity, power and thermal management and SiC photonics

$KLIC - wire bonding company to Advanced Packaging AI Bottleneck (Thermo compression bonding, HBM memory packaging, Chiplets, CPO)

All of the companies moved 80-100% in last 2 months. Not so crowded and not so talked about names. Might balance portfolio risk if anyone looking for similar returns but with a mature company. 

80% of the high flyers right now moved similar amounts (+/-15%) in last 2 months too.

And it’s compelling. The company is sampling quad 100G, quad 200G, and 32x50G NRZ uVCSELs next quarter, with high-volume ramp targeted for early 2027. The 200G parts clock in at 37GHz bandwidth, a meaningful threshold for 200G-per-lane operation. Importantly, PicoJool is building on one of the largest existing optical manufacturing ecosystems. The company’s thesis is that GaAs VCSEL capacity scales far more easily than the InP laser supply chains that many investors have focused on. PicoJool is building on GaAs with WIN Semiconductor, which has shipped over a billion VCSELs for 3D sensing applications. This decision to stay on GaAs instead of chasing indium phosphide or silicon photonics is important. GaAs VCSEL manufacturing capacity is vastly larger than merchant InP laser capacity. I'm also interested in the "slow and wide" option. The 32x50G uVCSEL isn't trying to push a single lane to extreme speeds. It runs 32 lanes at 50G NRZ, a massively parallel approach that fits naturally into co-packaged optics and near-packaged optics architectures. The cost target is to compete with copper which makes sense for AI scale-up interconnect, where you're wiring hundreds of GPUs inside a rack. The founder has done this before, too. Al Yuen shipped the first GbE VCSELs in 1996. At Alvesta, his first startup, he invented the active optical cable in 2001, the now-standard format where the E/O conversion lives inside the cable assembly rather than a separate pluggable module. Alvesta was acquired by Emcore in 2002. He went on to develop vertical oxidation techniques for high-volume VCSEL fabrication in 2016. Roadmap goes to 3.2T. 他们确认了年报和零分红，他们通过2025财年的损益表和资产负债表,并明确决议不向2025财年分配任何股息，再投资增长的逻辑确认。董事会与CEO获免责。 2. 他们确立了董事会改组，连任Bami Bastani会继续任董事长、Karin Raj、Todd Thomson;并且他们新增两位董事Joakim Nideborn担任副董事长)和Helena Svancar，这正是我们之前关注的AGM董事会变动。 董事薪酬方案包括年度津贴+股票持有计划：每人100万瑞典克朗,约一半用于购买Sivers股票并锁定1年，这一点能说明现有董事对于公司的长期看好！ 另外董事长薪酬105万克朗、副董事长60万克朗、其他董事各35万克朗,各委员会主席/成员另有额度。董事长将获得美国医疗保险覆盖这从侧面印证公司国际化和赴美布局。 这第二点我们完全可以看出来公司对于 $sive股价的决心和公司转变为美国企业的布局进程，美国医疗保险只会覆盖给长期留在美国的人，换句话说你想留在美国你才会去买美国医疗保险。 他们用自己的一半薪酬去购买 $sive这一点真的很值得敬佩！ 3. 他们的审计师Deloitte连任。 4. 按瑞典公司法批准董事会薪酬报告。 5. 他们增发授权这里15%稀释上限，这也是这一次的核心议案，授权董事会在下届AGM前,可一次或多次发行不超过5384万9956股普通股我们换算后对应稀释后约15%股本的新股/认股权证/可转债,可不按比例分配给现有股东,价格按发行时市场价，这一段比较重要，没有低价稀释。 这一条的重点在于：这是一个”备用授权”,目前没有具体执行计划,不代表立即稀释，这意味着目前没有任何稀释压力，并且就算要稀释我个人判断也是在股价上涨时。 6.在可转债方面，327k可转债是确认无误。 批准3月3日董事会决议,通过Bootstrap Europe 4.0认购622,719份面值4.77瑞典克朗的可转债,合计32.7万美元,年利率10.85%,2029年底到期(若未提前转股)。 这次AGM基本都是”走完法律流程”性质的确认,核心议案此前都已预告,市场大概率已price in。没有出现否决案、审计师更换、或临时新增的负面议案,本身就是一个”排雷成功”的信号，尤其考虑到此前空头报告、内幕交易调查等舆论压力下,股东大会顺利通过所有议案,本身对短期情绪是个利好。所以我们没有听到什么超预期的消息也没有听到纳斯达克的任何消息是很正常的。 很棒，在现在外面全是谣言和诽谤的情况下，这些决议都顺利通过了！ 非常好！特别是零分红+再投资逻辑获正式确认,符合我们对于”成长期公司应该把现金投入CPO/InP产能扩张”的判断框架。 #SIVE #SiversSemiconductors #AGM #股东大会 #CPO #硅光 #InP激光器 #GlobalFoundries #SCALE #AI infrastructure #光通信 text:
🔔 The Daily Semi-Cap — June 15, 2026

📊 SMH: $647.10 (+4.38%)

📈 Earnings:
• No new semiconductor earnings released today.

🔥 Major Developments:
• Qualcomm has been in talks to acquire Tenstorrent, with a discussed price around $8-10B. The read-through is straightforward: Qualcomm wants more AI-chip architecture talent and another shot at pushing beyond smartphones into custom compute.
• PicoJool announced 200G VCSELs and MicroVCSELs for AI scale-up links, with quad 100G/200G and 32x50G parts sampling next quarter and high-volume ramp targeted for early 2027. The interesting angle is GaAs scale: a mature VCSEL supply chain trying to compete with copper for dense short-reach GPU interconnects.
• IQE signed a multi-year InP epiwafer supply agreement with Tower Semiconductor tied to Tower’s silicon photonics platform. The agreement covers 200G/lane pluggables, next-gen 400G/lane modulators, and optical-circuit-switch applications, adding another data point that AI optical capacity is getting locked up years ahead.
• Semis ripped as the AI-infrastructure tape went full risk-on: SMH closed +4.38%, with memory and accelerator-adjacent names leading. The move keeps confirming that investors are still treating AI hardware, HBM, custom silicon, and optical networking as the cleanest places to be when macro pressure eases.

text:
$IQE deep dive:

FY2025 revenue was about GBP 97M, down 17.6% YoY. Photonics was GBP 57.1M, up 15%, while wireless was GBP 40.1M, down 40%. The latest yfinance snapshot had GBP 97.3M TTM revenue, 1.39% gross margin, negative EBITDA around GBP 8.2M, GBP 15.7M cash and GBP 91.7M debt.

The thesis is upstream photonics scarcity. IQE makes compound-semiconductor epiwafers/materials, including InP/GaN/GaAs, not the sexy final optical module. If AI data centers keep pushing 800G/1.6T optics and future co-packaged optics, the chokepoint can move upstream to hard-to-replicate wafer and epitaxy capacity.

The new catalyst is Tower Semiconductor. IQE signed a multi-year InP epiwafer supply agreement for AI-driven optical connectivity, including Tower's 200Gb/s-per-lane pluggable platform, next-gen 400Gb/s-per-lane modulator prototyping, and optical-circuit-switch work. It includes a year-one minimum purchase commitment and later minimum volume commitments. That is way better than "trust me bro, AI optics."

Second catalyst: MACOM's strategic investment and long-term supply agreements tied to the GBP 81M fundraising. That cleaned up some balance-sheet stress and validated the capacity. Third: the old consensus path was only about GBP 117M FY2026 revenue and GBP 140M FY2027 revenue, so any real AI/datacenter mix disclosure or guide raise would matter.

Risks are not cute. The stock has already ripped from distressed levels to 57.71GBX. Fully diluted market cap is roughly GBP 750M on sub-GBP 100M revenue and barely-there margins. New shares/convertibles diluted holders. HSBC covenant waiver history says the old balance sheet was fragile. And strategic customers can secure supply without giving IQE huge pricing power.

Entry/Verdict: CATALYST WATCH / optional tiny scout only, not a normal buy. I would not add size here after the vertical rerate. Clean entry is a pullback toward/under 30p with the thesis intact, or paying up only after Tower/MACOM revenue, backlog, guide, gross margin, or estimate revisions actually move.

This is real proof, not just photonics basket noise. But the market is already asking IQE to become strategic scarcity, not a cyclical wafer supplier with a cool press release.

We'll see how it goes.

text:
The biggest sectors in the market five years from now will be these. Position early and let the decade do the work.

AI Infrastructure. The backbone everything runs on. The data centers, the compute, the owned infrastructure powering every model on earth. Every other sector on this list runs on top of it.

Space. Connectivity and infrastructure moving off the planet and into orbit. Direct to phone networks, launch, and satellite constellations turning space into the next commercial frontier.

Photonics. The plumbing that moves the data. As clusters scale to hundreds of thousands of GPUs, moving information at the speed of light becomes the constraint. A market tripling this decade as 800G goes to 1.6T goes to 3.2T.

Nuclear Energy. The only power source that can actually feed AI at scale. Data centers need enormous, constant, carbon free baseload, and the grid cannot deliver it fast enough. Nuclear is the answer the entire industry is racing toward.

Memory. The bottleneck nobody can build fast enough. Every AI server needs 8 to 10 times the memory of a normal one, the makers are sold out, and the shortage runs for years. No memory, no AI.

Humanoid Robotics. AI stepping out of the screen and into the physical world. A market going from under $2 billion today to $38 billion by 2035. The companies making the brains, eyes, and sensors win no matter which robot wins.

Quantum Computing. The frontier that breaks the limits of classical computing entirely. Still early, still small, but governments and the biggest tech companies on earth are pouring billions in. The most asymmetric bet of the group.

Seven sectors. One direction. This is where the wealth of the next decade gets created, and we are early in every single one.

text:
You're reading about optics. You dive deep into photonics. You're wondering wth is Gan-on-Sci...

But you're missing one of the biggest chokepoints of all.

At sub-5nm, silicon dioxide fails. Electrons tunnel through it. The gate leaks. You need hafnium oxide — deposited atom by atom, precursor chemistry doing work no photolithography machine can substitute.

That's the chokepoint. And $4369.T (Tri Chemical Laboratories) owns the master patents.

Two challenges filed by Merck — one of the world's largest specialty chemical companies — to invalidate those patents. Both defeated. The moat isn't asserted. It survived industrial warfare.

Samsung recently confirmed a sourcing shift toward SK trichem, a JV where Tri Chemical holds 35% equity and the underlying IP. That's the thesis crystallizing in real time — not through a press release, but through actual procurement decisions.

Q1 FY2026: operating profit ¥2.07B, up 20.8% YoY. H1 FY2026 printed record-high net sales. The Minami-Alps facility is now in build-out — expanding into next-gen etching materials while hafnium volume ramps for 2nm production.

Here's why nobody's watching: $4369.T trades on the Tokyo exchange with essentially zero English-language institutional coverage. Western fintwit has no awareness of this name.

Price: ~¥3,980. 52-week range ¥2,455–4,455. Currently 11% off ATH, consolidating above prior highs. Not parabolic. Clean structure.

The 2nm ramp is 12–18 months from meaningful production volume. The precursor chemistry gets designed in first — that's already happening. By the time the node-shrink narrative reaches Western retail, the supply contracts are signed.

Find the bottleneck before the narrative does.

text:
Honestly, for me, photonics and memory are a safe bet

Memory means more profitability and it's safer in the short term

But lower returns in the long term

Photonics is more volatile and less profitable in the short term

But with much higher returns in the long term

I could full port $MU $WDC $SIVE and $AAOI for the next few years

And know for a fact that my portfolio is going to multiply

text:
Everyone wants to find the next $SNDK

I believe the next theme that will take off like memory will be anything related to photonics 

Here are my top 10 photonics stocks:

1) $AAOI - Optical transceivers
2) $AEHR - Chip testing systems
3) $LITE - Optical components
4) $COHR - Lasers & optics
5) $AVGO - AI semiconductors
6) $MRVL - Data center chips
7) $AXTI - Photonics materials
8) $CIEN - Optical networking gear
9) $SIVE - Precision timing chips
10) $CRDO - High speed connectivity

text:
Tower Semiconductor and Marvell Ship Over Five Million Coherent Photonic ICs
> Tower has collaborated with Marvell to advance next-generation coherent technologies, including process capabilities, such as the integration of non-silicon materials, the 3D integration of electronics and the enablement of advanced optical packaging such as V-Grooves, which serve to extend the performance and functionality of silicon photonics platforms.
> Coherent PICs are complex because they must control the phase and polarization of light, not just the amplitude. The design and process requirements for these PICs are far more stringent compared to simpler direct-detect chips.

text:
2026-06-18 半导体供应链瓶颈每日观察

核心判断：今天没有改变半导体供应链瓶颈大排序的强证据，但 AMKR/美国本土先进封装在社区里升温，值得进入明日验证清单；强证据层仍由 HBM、CoWoS/SoIC/先进封装设备和 InP epiwafer 线索支撑。

1. 今日最重要强证据仍不是新公告，而是对已有硬证据的延续复核。TSMC 月营收继续支持 AI 先进制程和先进封装景气，但还不能直接拆成 CoWoS/SoIC 产能新增；IQE/Tower 多年 InP epiwafer 协议仍是本周 CPO/硅光上游材料最强官方信号；NVIDIA Vera Rubin 与 Spectrum-X Ethernet Photonics 继续支持硅光方向；Micron HBM4 和 ASMPT C2W TCB repeat order 分别支撑 HBM 与先进封装设备层。

2. 对瓶颈层级排序的影响：大排序不变。第一梯队仍是 HBM/先进 DRAM、CoWoS/SoIC/2.5D/3D 先进封装、先进封装设备；CPO/硅光/光纤保持第二梯队上沿；InP epiwafer / InP laser / 硅光上游材料维持上调后的高跟踪优先级；glass substrate、panel-level packaging、CoPoS 和高端基板材料仍是中长期线索，暂不提前当成 2026 业绩弹性。

3. 标的跟踪权重变化：TSMC、HBM 三家、ASMPT/BESI/DISCO/测试设备这条主线不下调。IQE、Tower、Coherent、Lumentum、GF、Broadcom/Marvell 光链继续加权，但等待金额、volume、客户和产能数据。AMKR 小幅上调到验证清单，原因是社区重新把它看作美国 advanced packaging/test 基础设施资产，而不仅是传统 OSAT；但目前只有 Arizona campus 和与 TSMC 合作叙事，缺客户、订单、产能爬坡、收入拆分和经济性。

4. 社区观点是否改变判断：只改变细分跟踪优先级，不改变一级排序。AMKR 是今天最值得跟的新社区线索，但证据强度仍是 Weak/Medium；Nokia advanced packaging 仍未被官方材料确认，不能升级；SOCAMM vs HBM4 的分歧继续作为反方验证问题，不能把 CPU-side LPDDR5X/SOCAMM 配置变化直接读成 GPU-side HBM4 需求逆转。

5. 下一步验证信号：第一，查 AMKR Arizona 与 TSMC/客户的真实绑定、产能时间表、capex、先进封装收入和现金流回报。第二，继续拆 IQE/Tower 协议的最低采购、volume、定价、Tower 客户、IQE 产能和毛利。第三，等 TSMC 6 月营收和 Q2 法说会，看 CoWoS/SoIC/3DFabric 是否有更清晰拆分。第四，验证 Micron/SK hynix/Samsung 的 HBM4 份额、良率、ASP、capacity sold-out 和客户长约。第五，继续查 glass substrate/CoPoS 的量产时间、良率、成本和 2026 订单弹性。

"Tm90ZVR3ZWV0OjIwNjc3Mjc3NDUzNzU5ODU2NjQ=", "media": {"inline_media": []}, "richtext": {"richtext_tags": []}, "text": "2026-06-18 半导体供应链瓶颈每日观察 核心判断：今天没有改变半导体供应链瓶颈大排序的强证据，但 AMKR/美国本土先进封装在社区里升温，值得进入明日验证清单；强证据层仍由 HBM、CoWoS/SoIC/先进封装设备和 InP epiwafer 线索支撑。 1. 今日最重要强证据仍不是新公告，而是对已有硬证据的延续复核。TSMC 月营收继续支持 AI 先进制程和先进封装景气，但还不能直接拆成 CoWoS/SoIC 产能新增；IQE/Tower 多年 InP epiwafer 协议仍是本周 CPO/硅光上游材料最强官方信号；NVIDIA Vera Rubin 与 Spectrum-X Ethernet Photonics 继续支持硅光方向；Micron HBM4 和 ASMPT C2W TCB repeat order 分别支撑 HBM 与先进封装设备层。 2. 对瓶颈层级排序的影响：大排序不变。第一梯队仍是 HBM/先进 DRAM、CoWoS/SoIC/2.5D/3D 先进封装、先进封装设备；CPO/硅光/光纤保持第二梯队上沿；InP epiwafer / InP laser / 硅光上游材料维持上调后的高跟踪优先级；glass substrate、panel-level packaging、CoPoS 和高端基板材料仍是中长期线索，暂不提前当成 2026 业绩弹性。 3. 标的跟踪权重变化：TSMC、HBM 三家、ASMPT/BESI/DISCO/测试设备这条主线不下调。IQE、Tower、Coherent、Lumentum、GF、Broadcom/Marvell 光链继续加权，但等待金额、volume、客户和产能数据。AMKR 小幅上调到验证清单，原因是社区重新把它看作美国 advanced packaging/test 基础设施资产，而不仅是传统 OSAT；但目前只有 Arizona campus 和与 TSMC 合作叙事，缺客户、订单、产能爬坡、收入拆分和经济性。 4. 社区观点是否改变判断：只改变细分跟踪优先级，不改变一级排序。AMKR 是今天最值得跟的新社区线索，但证据强度仍是 Weak/Medium；Nokia advanced packaging 仍未被官方材料确认，不能升级；SOCAMM vs HBM4 的分歧继续作为反方验证问题，不能把 CPU-side LPDDR5X/SOCAMM 配置变化直接读成 GPU-side HBM4 需求逆转。 5. 下一步验证信号：第一，查 AMKR Arizona 与 TSMC/客户的真实绑定、产能时间表、capex、先进封装收入和现金流回报。第二，继续拆 IQE/Tower 协议的最低采购、volume、定价、Tower 客户、IQE 产能和毛利。第三，等 TSMC 6 月营收和 Q2 法说会，看 CoWoS/SoIC/3DFabric 是否有更清晰拆分。第四，验证 Micron/SK hynix/Samsung 的 HBM4 份额、良率、ASP、capacity sold-out 和客户长约。第五，继续查 glass substrate/CoPoS 的量产时间、良率、成本和 2026 订单弹性。"}}}, "rest_id": "2067727745514397989", text:
Co ty bredzisz, synku?

Co ma M120 Rak wspólnego z Rosomakiem, oprócz podwozia?

Wieża Raka to w pełni polski projekt, to najlepszy technologicznie moździerz samobieżny na świecie.

Wieża ZSSW-30? Najlepsza wieża bezzałogowa na świecie.

Gdzie produkujecie swoje FlyEye i Warmate? A, czekaj, to polska spółka produkuje je na Ukrainie.

To może polsko-fiński ICEYE? Może polska konstelacja Piast i Mikroglob od Creotech?

Gdzie wasze Vigo Photonics? Może chociaż macie swojego Topaza czy dalej korzystacie z polskich rozwiązań?

Wasz Flaming to trochę lepsze V2 z używanymi silnikami z lat 60. Dlatego "produkujecie" tak śmieszne liczby. Po prostu macie ich tyle ile się uda znaleźć 60letnich silników.

text:
Copper is dead. AI data centers need light — and photonics companies can’t build fast enough.

My deepdive on $AAOI & why this could be one of the best buying opportunities 

https://t.co/AbuYzpolre… https://t.co/WVl6onQCSl

Cpo를 알려준분

1️⃣ Serenity는 누구인가 (Fact)
• X 핸들: @aleabitoreddit
• Bio: “AI/Semi Supply Chain Analyst, former RISC-V FDN and AI research scientist. Now publishing free research on AI chokepoints.”
• 과거 Reddit WSB에서 AXTI 공급망 분석으로 유명해진 후 X로 이동.
• 특징: 초기 아이디어에 강한 반대(백래시)를 받지만, 이후 실적·기관 매수·파트너십으로 검증되는 패턴 반복.
• 최근: X 수익 공유 프로그램에서 2주마다 1억 뷰 이상 기록. 한국 주식(NAV 디스카운트 종목) 언급으로도 주목.

2️⃣ 핵심 투자 철학: Chokepoint Theory (Fact)
• “Big Tech(NVDA, MSFT 등)를 사지 말고, 그들이 반드시 의존할 수밖에 없는 물리적 병목(chokepoint)을 찾아라.”
• 방법: AI 데이터센터 → GPU/HBM/Optics 공급망을 바텀업으로 역설계 → 대체 불가능하거나 공급이 극도로 제한된 지점 식별.
• 주요 기준 (Fact 기반으로 자주 언급):
  - Certain Demand (명확한 메가트렌드 수요)
  - Constrained Supply (높은 진입장벽 + 희소 용량)
  - Low Market Attention (시장에서 아직 저평가/무시)
  - Value Capture potential (가격 결정력·점유율 확대 여지)
  - Catalyst (근시일 내 재평가 촉매)

• 대표 테마: Photonics / Silicon Photonics / CPO, InP substrate & laser, Advanced Packaging, Power semi, Memory bottleneck, mlcc등

3️⃣ 대표 사례와 검증 과정 (Fact / Estimate 구분)
Fact:
• AXTI: InP substrate 공급망 분석으로 WSB에서 밴 당할 정도로 반대 받음 → 이후 Reuters·기관 검증, 주가 대폭 상승.
• SIVE: 본인 최고 conviction 아이디어 중 하나. 스웨덴 미디어·일부에서 meme 취급 → Fidelity·JP Morgan 기관 매수 + JBL·GFS 파트너십 공식 발표로 검증.
• AAOI: $30 부근에서 “경영진 scam” 비판 받음 → 이후 광학 모듈 수요 확대와 함께 큰 상승.
• Memory 섹터 (MU, SK Hynix 등): 3개월 전 99% bearish 분위기 → 실제 대규모 상승.

Estimate:
• 정확한 개인 수익률 수치(일부 기사에서 225x 또는 연 +122% 등 언급)는 본인 공식 확인된 Fact 아님. 시장이 최종 검증한다는 입장 강조.
• 현재 아이디어(XFAB, 한국 NAV 디스카운트 종목 등)도 아직 진행 중으로, 추가 검증 필요.

4️⃣ 최근 활동 (2026년 6월 기준, Fact)
• X 수익 공유 프로그램 성과 공개 (2주마다 1억+ 뷰).
• 한국 주식 관심: Daeduck, DB HiTek 관련 NAV 디스카운트, Hyosung 등 언급.
• XFAB: Optical AI DC + Power semi 노출이 있지만 마케팅이 아쉽다는 코멘트.
• Photonics 강조 지속: AMD의 CW laser 확보, SIVE·AAOI 등 독립 용량 중요성.
• 전체적으로 “초기 반대 → 시장 검증” 패턴을 반복 강조.

━━━━━━━━

5️⃣ AI 인프라 공급망 관점에서 의미 (CPO·Photonics 중심)
• 현재 AI 데이터센터 빌드아웃에서 전력 효율·대역폭이 핵심 병목 → CPO(Co-Packaged Optics), InP 기반 laser·substrate, advanced packaging이 핵심 chokepoint로 부상.
• Serenity가 자주 언급하는 AXTI·SIVE·AAOI·COHR·XFAB 등은 CPO·포토닉스·특화 파운드리 영역과 직접 오버랩.
• “Big Tech 대신 upstream bottleneck” 접근은 단기 모멘텀과 달리, 구조적 공급 제약을 선점하려는 전략.

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

🟢 Base Case (현재 가시성 기준)
AI capex가 가이던스 수준으로 유지될 경우, 주요 chokepoint 기업들은 실적 성장과 기관 관심 확대에 따라 점진적 재평가 가능. 초기 반대 의견이 잦아들면서 일부 종목의 밸류에이션 정상화 진행.

🐂 Bull Case
AI 수요가 가속되고 여러 chokepoint(Photonics + Power + Memory 등)가 동시에 검증될 경우, 시장 주목이 낮았던 소형·중형 공급망 기업들이 비대칭적 upside를 기록할 수 있음. Serenity가 제시한 아이디어가 지속적으로 시장을 앞서가는 패턴 강화.

🐻 Bear Case
AI capex 둔화 또는 빅테크의 수직통합·신규 공급자 등장으로 용량 제약이 예상보다 빠르게 해소될 경우, 소형 chokepoint 기업들의 재평가가 지연되거나 valuation pressure 발생 가능. 매크로·유동성 악화 시 소형주 변동성 확대 리스크.

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

※ 이 글은 Serenity의 공개 포스팅과 관련 기사·트래커 사이트(https://t.co/qKzsAqeFlD)를 Fact 중심으로 정리한 분석입니다. 
투자 매수·매도 추천이 아니며, 특정 종목을 사거나 팔라고 권유하는 내용이 아닙니다. 
모든 투자 판단은 본인 책임 하에 충분히 검토하고 결정하시기 바랍니다. DYODD.

Now publishing free research on AI chokepoints.” • 과거 Reddit WSB에서 AXTI 공급망 분석으로 유명해진 후 X로 이동. • 특징: 초기 아이디어에 강한 반대(백래시)를 받지만, 이후 실적·기관 매수·파트너십으로 검증되는 패턴 반복. • 최근: X 수익 공유 프로그램에서 2주마다 1억 뷰 이상 기록. 한국 주식(NAV 디스카운트 종목) 언급으로도 주목. 2️⃣ 핵심 투자 철학: Chokepoint Theory (Fact) • “Big Tech(NVDA, MSFT 등)를 사지 말고, 그들이 반드시 의존할 수밖에 없는 물리적 병목(chokepoint)을 찾아라.” • 방법: AI 데이터센터 → GPU/HBM/Optics 공급망을 바텀업으로 역설계 → 대체 불가능하거나 공급이 극도로 제한된 지점 식별. • 주요 기준 (Fact 기반으로 자주 언급): - Certain Demand (명확한 메가트렌드 수요) - Constrained Supply (높은 진입장벽 + 희소 용량) - Low Market Attention (시장에서 아직 저평가/무시) - Value Capture potential (가격 결정력·점유율 확대 여지) - Catalyst (근시일 내 재평가 촉매) • 대표 테마: Photonics / Silicon Photonics / CPO, InP substrate & laser, Advanced Packaging, Power semi, Memory bottleneck, mlcc등 3️⃣ 대표 사례와 검증 과정 (Fact / Estimate 구분) Fact: • AXTI: InP substrate 공급망 분석으로 WSB에서 밴 당할 정도로 반대 받음 → 이후 Reuters·기관 검증, 주가 대폭 상승. • SIVE: 본인 최고 conviction 아이디어 중 하나. 스웨덴 미디어·일부에서 meme 취급 → Fidelity·JP Morgan 기관 매수 + JBL·GFS 파트너십 공식 발표로 검증. • AAOI: $30 부근에서 “경영진 scam” 비판 받음 → 이후 광학 모듈 수요 확대와 함께 큰 상승. • Memory 섹터 (MU, SK Hynix 등): 3개월 전 99% bearish 분위기 → 실제 대규모 상승. Estimate: • 정확한 개인 수익률 수치(일부 기사에서 225x 또는 연 +122% 등 언급)는 본인 공식 확인된 Fact 아님. 시장이 최종 검증한다는 입장 강조. • 현재 아이디어(XFAB, 한국 NAV 디스카운트 종목 등)도 아직 진행 중으로, 추가 검증 필요. 4️⃣ 최근 활동 (2026년 6월 기준, Fact) • X 수익 공유 프로그램 성과 공개 (2주마다 1억+ 뷰). • 한국 주식 관심: Daeduck, DB HiTek 관련 NAV 디스카운트, Hyosung 등 언급. • XFAB: Optical AI DC + Power semi 노출이 있지만 마케팅이 아쉽다는 코멘트. • Photonics 강조 지속: AMD의 CW laser 확보, SIVE·AAOI 등 독립 용량 중요성. • 전체적으로 “초기 반대 → 시장 검증” 패턴을 반복 강조. ━━━━━━━━ 5️⃣ AI 인프라 공급망 관점에서 의미 (CPO·Photonics 중심) • 현재 AI 데이터센터 빌드아웃에서 전력 효율·대역폭이 핵심 병목 → CPO(Co-Packaged Optics), InP 기반 laser·substrate, advanced packaging이 핵심 chokepoint로 부상. • Serenity가 자주 언급하는 AXTI·SIVE·AAOI·COHR·XFAB 등은 CPO·포토닉스·특화 파운드리 영역과 직접 오버랩. • “Big Tech 대신 upstream bottleneck” 접근은 단기 모멘텀과 달리, 구조적 공급 제약을 선점하려는 전략. ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━ 🟢 Base Case (현재 가시성 기준) AI capex가 가이던스 수준으로 유지될 경우, 주요 chokepoint 기업들은 실적 성장과 기관 관심 확대에 따라 점진적 재평가 가능. 초기 반대 의견이 잦아들면서 일부 종목의 밸류에이션 정상화 진행. 🐂 Bull Case AI 수요가 가속되고 여러 chokepoint(Photonics + Power + Memory 등)가 동시에 검증될 경우, 시장 주목이 낮았던 소형·중형 공급망 기업들이 비대칭적 upside를 기록할 수 있음. Serenity가 제시한 아이디어가 지속적으로 시장을 앞서가는 패턴 강화. 🐻 Bear Case AI capex 둔화 또는 빅테크의 수직통합·신규 공급자 등장으로 용량 제약이 예상보다 빠르게 해소될 경우, 소형 chokepoint 기업들의 재평가가 지연되거나 valuation pressure 발생 가능. 매크로·유동성 악화 시 소형주 변동성 확대 리스크. ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━ ※ 이 글은 Serenity의 공개 포스팅과 관련 기사·트래커 사이트(https://t.co/qKzsAqeFlD)를 Fact 중심으로 정리한 분석입니다. 투자 매수·매도 추천이 아니며, 특정 종목을 사거나 팔라고 권유하는 내용이 아닙니다. 모든 투자 판단은 본인 책임 하에 충분히 검토하고 결정하시기 바랍니다. text:
Digital precision guides, photonics compute and transport. Bypass the transistor, let light execute. 

💡🚀The von Neumann bottleneck is broken. We're moving from digital silicon to the effortless flow of Silicon Photonics [Lightmatter].

Read my article: https://t.co/mNzn5QxCF8

A must-watch video about the Lightmatter SiPho manufacturing process 🤩

From the Foundry, GlobalFoundries $GFS , to the ISE - ASE $ASX pre-packaging electrical and optical testing, to Amkor $AMKR for the packaging process, to again ISE - ASE for a post-packaging testing

Take note if you are an investor in Photonics $LITE $AAOI

Silicon photonics is the only way to connect hundreds of thousands of processors in Nvidia's Vera Rubin Ultra platform.

text:
The novel utility is: **you can now build a passive, single-shot, wide-band optical spectrometer with far fewer detector channels than conventional approaches require, on hardware that already exists.**

Breaking that down to its irreducible parts:

**The core utility:** A geometry (the log spiral) deterministically encodes delay/frequency into angle without any active processing. Once encoded, all the information sits on the chip at once — no scanning, no sweeping, one snapshot. You read it with a detector array placed at the right positions.

**Why that matters:** Detector pixels in certain bands (single-photon / infrared / THz) are scarce and expensive — each one is a cryogenic readout channel, a rare fabrication yield, a finite heat budget. To measure a wide band conventionally, you either (1) buy many pixels, or (2) scan serially with one fast detector. Both are constrained. The log spiral lets you (3) use far fewer pixels and still resolve the whole band, because the geometry did the sorting, not the pixel count.

**The concrete win:** On a 48-pixel SNSPD array (commercial ceiling), the log-spiral front end reaches resolving power R ≈ 31 across a 5-decade band (0.5–2.4 µm). A linear front end reaches R ≈ 10 on the same hardware, or needs ~3× more pixels (which don't exist as a single array) to match. That's 20× cheaper hardware for equal measurement quality.

**The novel part:** Nobody has connected this principle (geometry sorting timing into position) across vision, hearing, cortex dynamics, and engineered photonics before, and nobody has built it as a spectrometer. The idea that *passive geometry can do the computational work of an active optical component* — by itself — is what's new. It's not a tweak of existing dispersive instruments; it's a different category of operation.

**What it unlocks:** Any measurement that needs constant relative precision across a huge range, where detector pixels or data rate is the bottleneck, and where you can tolerate a snapshot (non-repeating signal). Single-photon spectroscopy, broadband mid-IR gas sensing, transient astrophysics, maybe quantum sensing. The enabling move is that it's *passive* — no power, no moving parts, no active tuning — which matters when your detector is already cryogenic and every watt counts.

**The honest boundary:** It doesn't beat all alternatives in all cases. Within a narrow band, or where you want absolute (not relative) precision, or where pixels are cheap (visible light), linear dispersion wins. The spiral is specialized. But for its corner — wide-band, expensive-pixel, single-shot regimes — it's not an optimization, it's the only path to the measurement at all.

That's the utility. Not "better spectrometer." More like: "a spectrometer that works where one didn't before."

text:
Ok, I'm done roasting big accounts that run subs

It is time for my honest take of accounts that I have subbed to

PAY ATTENTION because this post is worth about $100 of free insights (if you add up 1 month sub fee for all 4 subs I cover)

I am sharing this for free so that you can be better informed before subbing, as I don't think there is enough publicly available feedback or information on subs in general

Let's start with my favorite @jrouldz (subbed since late 2025, current sub)

🔹Has a main long term port
🔹Has a swing port
🔹The swing port is where most of the activity is in the sub
🔹Generally, new positions are added to the swing port with TP levels mapped out for swing traders. Doc himself often doesn't TP if he really believes in a position, he will let it run until it is large enough that he transfers it to his long term port
🔹Will provide trade alerts on entries
🔹Does weekly chart requests for subs
🔹Combination of technicals and fundamentals
🔹Recently started writing deep dive articles covering popular sectors/themes
🔹Very reasonable sub price of $12/mo
🔹Best for swing traders and building long term positions in speculative small cap stocks
🔹For beginners and advanced traders
🔹Overall relative value score: 9/10

Next up @crux_capital_ substack (subbed for < 1 month, current sub)

🔹Builds portfolios for themes
🔹Broad and deep industry coverage for each theme
🔹More expertise than anyone in photonics
🔹Building out physical AI portfolio now
🔹Mostly research and ER coverage, very few technicals but does give price targets and ideal entry zones
🔹Sub price of $30/mo or $300/year
🔹Value lies in research coverage if you don't have the time to research (most of the picks are not a secret after all)
🔹Best for long term holders and building thematic portfolios
🔹For intermediate/advanced investors
🔹Overall relative value score: 7.5/10

Now on to @StonkChris (subbed for 2 months)

🔹Posts more chart setups than anyone
🔹Uses Ichimoku clouds, Fibonacci, and RSI as primary indicators
🔹Picks standouts from each sector: energy, neoclouds, semis, space, etc
🔹Weekly chart requests
🔹Maps out smart money buy zones, which are the best prices to buy beaten down names such as crypto stocks, software, and neoclouds. This has often worked with much success
🔹Provides all the technical data but you need to decide which charts to buy
🔹Will occasionally give high conviction setup alerts but sometimes they don't play out
🔹No fundamental analysis, which will lead you to sell good stocks too early at the 1.618 or 2.0 fib levels based purely on technicals, e.g. $mrvl $NBIS 
🔹For advanced traders only (requires you to monitor dozens of stocks)
🔹Sub price of $20/month
🔹Overall relative value score: 6/10

Finally we have @market_sleuth (subbed for 1 month)

🔹Provides DMR (daily market report) 2-3 times a day
🔹Basically a WSJ pro trader subscription
🔹Covers indexes and Mag 7
🔹Used to provide trade alerts, not sure now
🔹Bias towards puts rather than calls on the $spy (mixed results)
🔹For intermediate/advanced traders
🔹More of a information only/macro view sub. You will need to determine your own trades for the most part
🔹Sub price of almost $30/month
🔹Overall relative value score: 3/10

I would like to note that all 4 of these accounts run successful subs and this is only my experience with the subs (others may have a different experience). Each of these accounts are great people and I hope they don't put too much weight on the score I gave them. Scores are based on sub price vs. value as a part time, not full time investor. You could argue that Chris and Sleuth deserve higher scores if you are a full time investor/trader and value the resource. Ultimately my score and decision is based on my part time investment/trading activity and the value I find in each sub.

Perhaps the ultimate hack is subbing to @jrouldz for market timing/small caps, @StonkChris for technicals, and @crux_capital_ for research/fundamentals? 🤔

text:
$SIVE 와 삼성전자 협력가능성 많다는데(AI)

$SIVE(Sivers Semiconductors)의 Photonics 사업부는 실리콘 포토닉스(SiPh)와 CPO에 최적화된 고출력 InP DFB 레이저 전문 기업입니다. 삼성전자와의 협력 가능성은 상당히 높게 평가됩니다(추정 60~80% 수준). 아래에 이유와 배경을 정리했습니다.
왜 Sivers가 관련이 있나?
•  SiPh의 최대 약점은 **레이저(광원)**입니다. 실리콘은 간접 밴드갭이라 레이저 발진이 어렵습니다. 그래서 대부분 InP(인듐인화물) 기반 III-V 레이저를 하이브리드 통합(flip-chip)하거나 External Light Source(ELS) 형태로 따로 붙입니다.
•  Sivers Photonics는 InP100 플랫폼으로 고출력 DFB 레이저 칩·어레이를 만들고, SiPh flip-chip hybrid integration에 최적화되어 있습니다.
•  AI/HPC 데이터센터용 고출력 레이저 (800G+ 트랜시버, CPO, CW-WDM MSA 호환)
•  4채널·8채널 어레이 등 멀티-파장 지원
•  고신뢰성·고수율·대량 생산 가능
이 기술이 바로 삼성이 2027년 CPO 상용화에서 필요한 광원 부분과 정확히 맞아떨어집니다.
삼성전자가 레이저를 직접 만들 가능성은?
매우 낮습니다. (사용자 의견에 전적으로 동의)
•  삼성은 실리콘 CMOS·패키징·HBM·어드밴스드 패키징에 강하지만, InP 기반 고출력·고신뢰성 레이저 기술은 완전히 다른 영역입니다.
•  신뢰성 검증·수율 안정화·대량 생산까지 걸리는 시간이 최소 수년 이상 소요됩니다.
•  삼성의 목표(2027년 CPO)가 매우 타이트하기 때문에, 레이저는 외부 전문사 공급 또는 공동개발이 가장 현실적인 선택입니다.
협력 가능성 평가
긍정적 요인 (가능성 ↑)
•  기술 보완성 최고: 삼성 = SiPh 플랫폼 + CPO 패키징(열 관리, 본딩 등) 강점 Sivers = 레이저(광원) 강점 → 완벽한 조합. Sivers는 이미 GlobalFoundries(GF)와 SiPh 플랫폼 협력을 발표하며 레이저를 GF SiPh에 통합하는 레퍼런스 디자인을 진행 중입니다. https://t.co/UV8OHUjtbB 
•  Sivers는 이미 Ayar Labs, POET Technologies 등 SiPh/CPO 플레이어와 레이저 공급·개발 계약을 맺고 있으며, hyperscaler(대형 AI 회사)와도 논의 중입니다.
•  삼성의 최근 움직임(싱가포르 R&D 확대, TSMC·인텔 인재 영입, Broadcom 협력 등)과 잘 맞습니다. 빠른 상용화를 위해 “레버리지(외부 기술 활용)” 전략을 쓰고 있음.
•  CPO 시장에서 ELS(외부 광원) 방식이 주류로 자리 잡고 있어, Sivers 같은 전문 레이저 공급사가 필요한 상황입니다.
부정적·리스크 요인 (가능성 ↓)
•  Sivers는 중소기업(시총 약 280억 SEK 수준)이라 대량 공급 안정성을 삼성이 걱정할 수 있습니다. (WIN Semiconductors와 생산 확대 협력 중이긴 함)
•  삼성이 더 큰 공급사(Lumentum, Coherent 등)나 Celestial AI 투자처럼 전략적 인수·투자를 병행할 가능성도 있음.
•  아직 공개된 직접 협력 소식은 없습니다.
종합 의견
삼성이 2027년 CPO를 제대로 밀어붙이려면 레이저 광원은 반드시 외부에서 빠르게 확보해야 합니다. Sivers는 기술적으로 가장 잘 맞는 후보 중 하나입니다.
가능한 협력 형태:
•  레이저 칩 공급 계약
•  공동 개발 (삼성 SiPh 플랫폼 + Sivers 레이저 최적화)
•  레퍼런스 디자인 공동 개발 (GF처럼)
시간이 촉박한 만큼 2026~2027년 안에 움직임이 있을 가능성이 높아 보입니다. (물론 아직은 추측 단계)

Photonics 사업부는 실리콘 포토닉스(SiPh)와 CPO에 최적화된 고출력 InP DFB 레이저 전문 기업입니다. 삼성전자와의 협력 가능성은 상당히 높게 평가됩니다(추정 60~80% 수준). 아래에 이유와 배경을 정리했습니다. 왜 Sivers가 관련이 있나? • SiPh의 최대 약점은 **레이저(광원)**입니다. 실리콘은 간접 밴드갭이라 레이저 발진이 어렵습니다. 그래서 대부분 InP(인듐인화물) 기반 III-V 레이저를 하이브리드 통합(flip-chip)하거나 External Light Source(ELS) 형태로 따로 붙입니다. • Sivers Photonics는 InP100 플랫폼으로 고출력 DFB 레이저 칩·어레이를 만들고, SiPh flip-chip hybrid integration에 최적화되어 있습니다. • AI/HPC 데이터센터용 고출력 레이저 (800G+ 트랜시버, CPO, CW-WDM MSA 호환) • 4채널·8채널 어레이 등 멀티-파장 지원 • 고신뢰성·고수율·대량 생산 가능 이 기술이 바로 삼성이 2027년 CPO 상용화에서 필요한 광원 부분과 정확히 맞아떨어집니다. 삼성전자가 레이저를 직접 만들 가능성은? 매우 낮습니다. (사용자 의견에 전적으로 동의) • 삼성은 실리콘 CMOS·패키징·HBM·어드밴스드 패키징에 강하지만, InP 기반 고출력·고신뢰성 레이저 기술은 완전히 다른 영역입니다. • 신뢰성 검증·수율 안정화·대량 생산까지 걸리는 시간이 최소 수년 이상 소요됩니다. • 삼성의 목표(2027년 CPO)가 매우 타이트하기 때문에, 레이저는 외부 전문사 공급 또는 공동개발이 가장 현실적인 선택입니다. 협력 가능성 평가 긍정적 요인 (가능성 ↑) • 기술 보완성 최고: 삼성 = SiPh 플랫폼 + CPO 패키징(열 관리, 본딩 등) 강점 Sivers = 레이저(광원) 강점 → 완벽한 조합. Sivers는 이미 GlobalFoundries(GF)와 SiPh 플랫폼 협력을 발표하며 레이저를 GF SiPh에 통합하는 레퍼런스 디자인을 진행 중입니다. https://t.co/UV8OHUjtbB • Sivers는 이미 Ayar Labs, POET Technologies 등 SiPh/CPO 플레이어와 레이저 공급·개발 계약을 맺고 있으며, hyperscaler(대형 AI 회사)와도 논의 중입니다. • 삼성의 최근 움직임(싱가포르 R&D 확대, TSMC·인텔 인재 영입, Broadcom 협력 등)과 잘 맞습니다. 빠른 상용화를 위해 “레버리지(외부 기술 활용)” 전략을 쓰고 있음. • CPO 시장에서 ELS(외부 광원) 방식이 주류로 자리 잡고 있어, Sivers 같은 전문 레이저 공급사가 필요한 상황입니다. 부정적·리스크 요인 (가능성 ↓) • Sivers는 중소기업(시총 약 280억 SEK 수준)이라 대량 공급 안정성을 삼성이 걱정할 수 있습니다. (WIN Semiconductors와 생산 확대 협력 중이긴 함) • 삼성이 더 큰 공급사(Lumentum, Coherent 등)나 Celestial AI 투자처럼 전략적 인수·투자를 병행할 가능성도 있음. • 아직 공개된 직접 협력 소식은 없습니다. 종합 의견 삼성이 2027년 CPO를 제대로 밀어붙이려면 레이저 광원은 반드시 외부에서 빠르게 확보해야 합니다. Sivers는 기술적으로 가장 잘 맞는 후보 중 하나입니다. 가능한 협력 형태: • 레이저 칩 공급 계약 • 공동 개발 (삼성 SiPh 플랫폼 + Sivers 레이저 최적화) • 레퍼런스 디자인 공동 개발 (GF처럼) 시간이 촉박한 만큼 2026~2027년 안에 움직임이 있을 가능성이 높아 보입니다. text:
IT/Tech: Gartner’s Top 10 Strategic Technology Trends for 2026 highlight AI-native development platforms, AI supercomputing, multiagent systems, domain-specific LLMs, and Physical AI. AI agents are moving from pilots to autonomous workflows across code, operations, and more. Preemptive cybersecurity and confidential computing are critical for trust. Companies are optimizing AI usage due to compute and power costs, with focus on sovereign AI, resilient supply chains, and exponential IT transformation. Broader momentum includes quantum, immersive reality, advanced semiconductors, and space/bio tech.

Communications/Telecom: 5G subscriptions reached 3.1 billion in Q1 2026, with uplink traffic growing faster than downlink for many providers. 5G Standalone slicing is expanding commercially. AI-native networks bring self-healing capabilities, intent-based orchestration, and automation for CX, fraud, and security. Telcos are turning physical assets into distributed AI grids. 6G preparation with multi-orbit satellites and terahertz research is accelerating, alongside growth in RCS, eSIM/MVNO, and Network as a Service models.

Fiber & Infrastructure: AI data center demand is driving massive fiber growth, with hyperscalers requiring high-density cables (1,000+ fibers per rack), shorter-reach priorities, CPO/NPO optics, and 800G+ transceivers. Fiber counts are quadrupling in deployments, with hollow-core and multicore innovations on the horizon. FTTH remains the endgame, with 87% of North American and European telcos actively deploying fiber. BEAD projects are ramping, and AI serves as a dual-use catalyst for both residential and hyperscale backhaul. High-count ribbon splicing, mass fusion mastery, OTDR/loss budgeting, and silicon photonics are key workflow shifts for AI-scale links.

#FiberOptics #AITechnology #5GNetworks #DataCenterInfra

text:
Photonics vs Copper 

This is a must read to understand server racks and how data centers are being constructed 

More importantly how Photonics like $SIVE has defined the downstream value chain

Here’s another visual from the article, illustrating a Blackwell GPU rack. Once copper reaches its limits, photonics take over.

• Copper based: $8B → $34B
• Optics based: $3B → $120B https://t.co/9bFwUqREF4

text:
I am probably stating the obvious here but these are the themes that have outperformed/underperformed YTD

- Semiconductors OUTPERFORMED
- Photonics OUTPERFORMED
- Neoclouds UNDERPERFORMED
- Software UNDERPERFORMED
- Space UNDERPERFORMED
- Quantum UNDERPERFORMED
- Nuclear UNDERPERFORMED
- Energy OUTPERFORMED
- Solar OUTPERFORMED
- Defense UNDERPERFORMED

Chances are if you didn’t have an overweight allocation to semiconductors this year, you underperformed

I think that there are opportunities in the underperforming sectors but the chances are the semiconductors continue outperforming

Semiconductors have always been a cyclical and momentum trade

And it doesn’t seem like the music is stopping anytime soon

text:
The whole market is obsessed with timing the photonics transition. 

The smartest way to play it is to own the company that wins whether it happens early or late.

That is $CRDO.

$CRDO core business is copper, not optics. Their active electrical cables own up to 88% of the AEC market and are the standard for connecting AI servers inside the rack. 

So when co packaged optics slips, and it just got pushed to 2028, copper stays dominant longer. The delay everyone fears actually extends Credo’s best business.

And they built optics in-house anyway, so they win that transition too whenever it finally comes.

That is the asymmetry. If photonics is early, $CRDO is ready with its optical stack. If photonics is late, Credo’s copper monopoly runs even longer. Heads they win, tails they win.

While everyone else is betting on when the shift happens, $CRDO gets paid either way.

My current portfolio map changed a bit, so here's the public version.

Core holdings:
AI neocloud / infra — $NBIS, IREN, OSS
Memory — MU, DRAM
AI software / workflow — CRWD, PLTR, NOW, TEAM
Fintech / crypto rails — HOOD, RDDT, LMND, IBIT, ETHA
Photonics and optical — LITE, COHR, GLW, AAOI
Power / energy transition — FLNC, ENPH, NVTS
Semi tools / packaging — PDFS, CAMT, ONTO, SANM, AOSL, MRAM
Perception / edge — OUST

Changes: added to ENPH, pressed metals call exposure, and added more NOW optionality. No new common-stock ticker bucket; the bigger change is risk mix.

Most of my research here is photonics, memory, power, and AI infra bottlenecks. The actual portfolio is broader because the market does not care about my neat little theme folders.

Still in HOLD / no broad press mode. We'll see which ones earn their way in

text:
Machvision The hidden inspection chokepoint of the CPO / advanced-packaging ramp TWSE: 3563

Here is my first short thesis on Machvision, including what I consider to be some of the most important information about the company.

Machvision already ships CoWoS and silicon-photonics inspection in commercial volume, pursues an explicit diversification “Under the leadership of ASE Group”, and sits on a captive base of ~90% of the world’s top-100 PCB makers yet the market still prices it as a cyclical PCB-AOI equipment name. That is the perception gap this note maps, with the filing evidence embedded.

Why the market is mispricing it

The Street still values a cyclical PCB-AOI equipment maker. The company’s own chairman and filings now describe something else: a firm becoming indispensable inspection infrastructure for the CPO and advanced-packaging volume ramp, anchored into ASE’s capital structure, with CoWoS and silicon-photonics inspection products already in commercial deployment. Like the AOI names the market does track (KLA, Camtek, Onto), Machvision is an inspection chokepoint but it maps directly and by name into ASE, the world’s largest OSAT, in a way the broader market has not priced.

The anchor ASE (documented, both sides)

Equity stake. ASE carries Machvision as an equity-method associate.

Carrying amount NT$2,120,140K (~US$67.5m) at 31 Dec 2025, up from NT$2,095,506K in 2024 (ASE 20-F,“Investments in associates”)

“Significant influence” related parties. ASE, ASE Electronics, ASE (Shanghai) and Siliconware Precision Industries (SPIL) the ASE subsidiary that, per JPMorgan, runs the CoPoS panel-level project for Nvidia (2028) are all named as entities with significant influence over Machvision.

Related-party revenue is exploding. ASE-group revenue to Machvision reached NT$273,117K in 9M-2025 vs NT$30,556K a year earlier (+793%), ≈10.8% of 9M-2025 revenue (NT$2,527m). A +793% jump is not the signature of an arm’s-length, multi-vendor relationship.

The partnership, in the company’s words. In 2024 Machvision invested in HYE to jointly develop semiconductor- and advanced-packaging AOI equipment “Under the leadership of ASE Group” (Machvision ESG-investor disclosure).

The captive base who Machvision works with

Machvision states ~90% of the 2023 global top-100 PCB makers are customers, and all top-10 global PCB makers. Named clients include Unimicron, Nan Ya, TSMC, ASE Group, Chin Poon, Tripod, Gold Circuit (TW); Foxconn, Founder, Shennan, Kinwong (CN); Kingboard (HK); Samsung, LG (KR); the top-2 Japanese makers; Sanmina (US). Those same customers are the ones expanding fastest into IC-substrate and advanced packaging Machvision up-sells into a captive base rather than chasing new logos. 

The structural pivot “dual-track, four-line”

Management’s own framing (2025 AGM; reaffirmed at the April-2026 Kunshan launch). Two sectors PCB and IC packaging/testing with AOI the core of both, across four lines:

PCB-AOI legacy; share of revenue declining by design, not from decline.

PCB Four-Wire Electrical Testing six years of R&D;, launched Q1-2026, a market historically dominated by Japanese suppliers. A fourth revenue line absent from analyst models.

Wafer inspection advanced packaging.

Package inspection traditional + advanced six-sided, CoWoS, and silicon-photonics inspection. The core of the thesis.

The chairman says the PCB-AOI share will shrink because the new lines grow faster, with R&D; deliberately focused on “gross margins exceeding 60%” products and above-mainstream specs (an explicit “no me-too” rule). That is the re-rating, in management’s own words.

The financial ramp it is in the numbers

Q1-2025 revenue NT$792m, +198% YoY a record (equipment +231%). By geography: Taiwan +262%; China NT$538.6m (~68%, +147%); “Others” (likely Thailand) NT$9m → NT$108m (+1,068%).

Contract liabilities (advance receipts) NT$138.2m (Mar-25) vs NT$16.4m (Mar-24) ×8.4. Customers pre-paying to reserve capacity before delivery the behaviour of a market where allocation is tightening (pricing-power signal).

April-2025 revenue NT$330m, +219.5% YoY; near-term semiconductor mix guided to 15–20% of sales.

Thailand factory purchased, following 20+ PCB makers relocating there active geopolitical mitigation.

Photonics the forgotten inspection layer

The AI → bandwidth → CPO transition (Nvidia Spectrum-X Photonics, Broadcom, Marvell; TSMC integrating optics into CoWoS; ASE doubling advanced-packaging sales toward ~US$3.2bn in 2026 and bringing up CoPoS for Nvidia in 2028) drives demand across the entire photonic/packaging chain lasers, assembly, packaging, and inspection. Inspection is the layer the market forgets because it is not in the final product; yet without it no volume ramp is possible.

The anchor verbatim. At the 2025 AGM the chairman stated the “Package inspection” line covers “advanced six-sided inspection for CoWoS … processes, and even silicon photonics inspection.” Not a roadmap a product in commercial deployment, corroborated by the April-2026 launch of AOI for FOPLP / FOWLP / silicon photonics.

ASE is a central CPO player (CoWoS/CoPoS for Nvidia 2028; the world’s #1 OSAT), so inspection “under ASE leadership” sits one hop from the CPO programs ASE serves. CoWoS sub-micron 3D, silicon-photonics on silicon, and glass-substrate/TGV (2027–28) all defeat conventional 2D AOI the gap Machvision’s 3D-measurement + LaserVia stack addresses.

The asymmetry

The “boring pivot” market prices a cyclical AOI/PCB maker; the chairman describes a transformation into indispensable CPO-ramp inspection infrastructure. The core perception gap.

Not “priced for perfection” Machvision is profitable with ~60% gross margin and a real earnings floor (caveat: already ~2x off the lows, so the asymmetry is narrower than it was).

Catalyst not yet modeled the Package-inspection CoWoS/SiPh line, the new Four-Wire Electrical Testing line, the ASE-CPO exposure, and the glass-substrate/TGV wave (2027–28) are not in Street models.

Thank you for taking the time to read this thesis. It is only a brief summary of the information I was able to find, but I hope I have been as clear and concise as possible. Please feel free to share your feedback. If I have overlooked any important points or made any mistakes, please let me know as well !

Machvision already ships CoWoS and silicon-photonics inspection in commercial volume, pursues an explicit diversification “Under the leadership of ASE Group”, and sits on a captive base of ~90% of the world’s top-100 PCB makers yet the market still prices it as a cyclical PCB-AOI equipment name. That is the perception gap this note maps, with the filing evidence embedded. Why the market is mispricing it The Street still values a cyclical PCB-AOI equipment maker. The company’s own chairman and filings now describe something else: a firm becoming indispensable inspection infrastructure for the CPO and advanced-packaging volume ramp, anchored into ASE’s capital structure, with CoWoS and silicon-photonics inspection products already in commercial deployment. Like the AOI names the market does track (KLA, Camtek, Onto), Machvision is an inspection chokepoint but it maps directly and by name into ASE, the world’s largest OSAT, in a way the broader market has not priced. The anchor ASE (documented, both sides) Equity stake. ASE carries Machvision as an equity-method associate. Carrying amount NT$2,120,140K (~US$67.5m) at 31 Dec 2025, up from NT$2,095,506K in 2024 (ASE 20-F,“Investments in associates”) “Significant influence” related parties. ASE, ASE Electronics, ASE (Shanghai) and Siliconware Precision Industries (SPIL) the ASE subsidiary that, per JPMorgan, runs the CoPoS panel-level project for Nvidia (2028) are all named as entities with significant influence over Machvision. Related-party revenue is exploding. ASE-group revenue to Machvision reached NT$273,117K in 9M-2025 vs NT$30,556K a year earlier (+793%), ≈10.8% of 9M-2025 revenue (NT$2,527m). A +793% jump is not the signature of an arm’s-length, multi-vendor relationship. The partnership, in the company’s words. In 2024 Machvision invested in HYE to jointly develop semiconductor- and advanced-packaging AOI equipment “Under the leadership of ASE Group” (Machvision ESG-investor disclosure). The captive base who Machvision works with Machvision states ~90% of the 2023 global top-100 PCB makers are customers, and all top-10 global PCB makers. Named clients include Unimicron, Nan Ya, TSMC, ASE Group, Chin Poon, Tripod, Gold Circuit (TW); Foxconn, Founder, Shennan, Kinwong (CN); Kingboard (HK); Samsung, LG (KR); the top-2 Japanese makers; Sanmina (US). Those same customers are the ones expanding fastest into IC-substrate and advanced packaging Machvision up-sells into a captive base rather than chasing new logos. The structural pivot “dual-track, four-line” Management’s own framing (2025 AGM; reaffirmed at the April-2026 Kunshan launch). Two sectors PCB and IC packaging/testing with AOI the core of both, across four lines: PCB-AOI legacy; share of revenue declining by design, not from decline. PCB Four-Wire Electrical Testing six years of R&D;, launched Q1-2026, a market historically dominated by Japanese suppliers. A fourth revenue line absent from analyst models. Wafer inspection advanced packaging. Package inspection traditional + advanced six-sided, CoWoS, and silicon-photonics inspection. The core of the thesis. The chairman says the PCB-AOI share will shrink because the new lines grow faster, with R&D; deliberately focused on “gross margins exceeding 60%” products and above-mainstream specs (an explicit “no me-too” rule). That is the re-rating, in management’s own words. The financial ramp it is in the numbers Q1-2025 revenue NT$792m, +198% YoY a record (equipment +231%). By geography: Taiwan +262%; China NT$538.6m (~68%, +147%); “Others” (likely Thailand) NT$9m → NT$108m (+1,068%). Contract liabilities (advance receipts) NT$138.2m (Mar-25) vs NT$16.4m (Mar-24) ×8.4. Customers pre-paying to reserve capacity before delivery the behaviour of a market where allocation is tightening (pricing-power signal). April-2025 revenue NT$330m, +219.5% YoY; near-term semiconductor mix guided to 15–20% of sales. Thailand factory purchased, following 20+ PCB makers relocating there active geopolitical mitigation. Photonics the forgotten inspection layer The AI → bandwidth → CPO transition (Nvidia Spectrum-X Photonics, Broadcom, Marvell; TSMC integrating optics into CoWoS; ASE doubling advanced-packaging sales toward ~US$3.2bn in 2026 and bringing up CoPoS for Nvidia in 2028) drives demand across the entire photonic/packaging chain lasers, assembly, packaging, and inspection. Inspection is the layer the market forgets because it is not in the final product; yet without it no volume ramp is possible. The anchor verbatim. At the 2025 AGM the chairman stated the “Package inspection” line covers “advanced six-sided inspection for CoWoS … processes, and even silicon photonics inspection.” Not a roadmap a product in commercial deployment, corroborated by the April-2026 launch of AOI for FOPLP / FOWLP / silicon photonics. ASE is a central CPO player (CoWoS/CoPoS for Nvidia 2028; the world’s #1 OSAT), so inspection “under ASE leadership” sits one hop from the CPO programs ASE serves. CoWoS sub-micron 3D, silicon-photonics on silicon, and glass-substrate/TGV (2027–28) all defeat conventional 2D AOI the gap Machvision’s 3D-measurement + LaserVia stack addresses. The asymmetry The “boring pivot” market prices a cyclical AOI/PCB maker; the chairman describes a transformation into indispensable CPO-ramp inspection infrastructure. The core perception gap. Not “priced for perfection” Machvision is profitable with ~60% gross margin and a real earnings floor (caveat: already ~2x off the lows, so the asymmetry is narrower than it was). Catalyst not yet modeled the Package-inspection CoWoS/SiPh line, the new Four-Wire Electrical Testing line, the ASE-CPO exposure, and the glass-substrate/TGV wave (2027–28) are not in Street models. Thank you for taking the time to read this thesis. It is only a brief summary of the information I was able to find, but I hope I have been as clear and concise as possible. Please feel free to share your feedback. If I have overlooked any important points or made any mistakes, please let me know as well !"}}}, "rest_id": "2068813883507114008", "source": "Twitter Web App", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "219", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}

text:
🤖 US startup starts work on ‘first utility-scale quantum computer’ in Australia

American startup PsiQuantum has begun construction on a facility in Moreton Bay, Australia, where it plans to build and deploy what it calls the world’s first utility-scale, fault-tolerant quantum computer.

Unlike many quantum computing efforts that remain focused on laboratory-scale systems, PsiQuantum is pursuing a photonics-based approach designed to scale using existing semiconductor manufacturing processes and optical networking technologies.

“Building a quantum computer that solves real world problems is one of the great engineering challenges of our time. For decades, quantum computing has held the promise of transforming what humanity can achieve through computation, and today in Australia we are beginning to turn that promise into reality,” — said Victor Peng, CEO of PsiQuantum.

The company believes fault-tolerant quantum computers could eventually tackle problems beyond the reach of conventional computing systems. Potential applications include drug discovery, materials development, energy research, logistics, manufacturing, agriculture, and financial modeling.

text:
$MRVL just detailed a major leap in silicon photonics for the AI era. 🔥

Conventional photonic modulators are hitting the diffraction limit of light—they can’t scale fast enough for the insane bandwidth AI clusters will demand.

Plasmonics changes the game. By squeezing light below the diffraction limit at a metal-dielectric interface, Marvell’s team is unlocking speeds >1 THz (10x+ faster than today’s best photonic modulators), devices only ~10 microns long (300–500x smaller), and exponentially higher bandwidth density all inside the existing silicon photonics platform.

No need for exotic new materials. Just smarter physics.

This directly powers the optical interconnects for next-gen scale-up and scale-across AI networks, exactly where Marvell has been building leadership (custom silicon + high-speed optics). They’ve already delivered high-speed SiPho devices with plasmonic tech in 2026, with the roadmap pointing to 3.2T+ modules and big wins in power and space efficiency.

This foundational tech compounds over 5–10 years as AI infrastructure scales. Long-term holders know: connectivity is becoming as critical as the accelerators themselves.

text:
$ALMU - THE PUBLIC PROXY FOR PHOTONICS YOU CAN'T BUY IN ANDURIL OR LIGHTMATTER, STILL PRE-REVENUE

Aeluma rose ~6% to $25 as the AI-optics complex snapped back from yesterday's rate flush. Its pitch: manufacturing III-V compound semiconductors — the lasers and detectors photonics needs — directly on 300mm silicon wafers with CMOS-compatible processes, partnered with Tower Semiconductor and Sumitomo, with quantum-dot lasers aimed at data-center co-packaged optics.

Be honest about the stage: fiscal-2026 revenue is guided to just $4.2–4.6M from R&D contracts, not production hardware, with no named commercial design win yet. This is a call option on an inevitability — the bet that scalable III-V-on-silicon is the missing manufacturing layer when CPO goes volume — not a revenue story today.

text:
$AEHR - THE BURN-IN BOTTLENECK NOBODY PRICES UNTIL AI CHIPS START FAILING IN THE FIELD

Aehr Test Systems rose ~7% to $113 as investors re-rated its wafer-level burn-in franchise into a William Blair presentation and a fresh June deck mapping AI-processor and data-center demand. The company already booked a record $41M production order from a leading hyperscale AI customer this spring and is now chasing a development deal for HBM4E memory-optimized blades, targeting fiscal-2027 orders.

The mechanism is reliability at scale: as SiC, GaN, photonics and HBM go into multi-thousand-GPU racks, every die has to be stressed before it ships — and Aehr is the lone pure-play in wafer-level burn-in across all four, the second derivative of the AI buildout.

text:
$NOK

Nokia is a screaming buy right now

The company has unveiled plans to expand its photonic chip facility in Allentown, Pennsylvania with the additional capacity expected to be operational by the end of Q3 2026

The fact that you can still buy this stock at $13 tells me the market still treats this company as a mobile phone business 

Nokia has taken control of one of the most significant photonics manufacturing facilities in the United States and is now driving a tenfold scale up that is backed by government funding

This stock should easily be $30

text:
🔔 The Daily Semi-Cap — June 17, 2026

📊 SMH: $623.97 (+1.29%)

📈 Earnings:
• JBL — $8.8B rev (est $8.66B), $3.16 core EPS. Core operating margin 5.7%, +12% YoY. Raised FY26 outlook to $35B revenue, 5.8% core operating margin, $12.70 core EPS, and $1.4B+ adjusted FCF as AI infrastructure demand stayed extremely strong. Stock finished +1.9%.

🔥 Major Developments:
• AEHR received another follow-on production order for a fully automated FOX-XP wafer-level burn-in system from a major silicon photonics customer serving hyperscale data-center optical interconnects, with additional systems forecast this year as next-gen capacity ramps.
• Intel’s 18A-P process entered risk production, moving its most advanced foundry node closer to external customer qualification and keeping the Apple/advanced-node second-source debate alive.
• NVIDIA Blackwell added day-zero support for MiniMax M3, a 428B-parameter MoE model with 1M-token context, reinforcing the push toward high-throughput agentic inference on Blackwell systems.
• AI infrastructure breadth kept widening: Jabil raised its FY26 outlook on hyperscale demand, while Aehr’s optical burn-in order points to production bottlenecks moving deeper into photonics reliability, test, and manufacturing capacity.

text:
From raw materials to finished systems, here’s how the entire photonics stack is positioned to benefit from exploding demand in AI data centers, optical networking, and high-speed computing.

Layer 1 - Materials & Wafers
 $GLW $AXTI $IQE $AIXA $AMS

Layer 2 - Core Photonic Devices
 $IPGP $COHR $LITE $LASR $SIVE

Layer 3 - Components & Modules
 $AAOI $MTSI $FN $VIAV $LPTH

Layer 4 - Systems & Equipment
 $ASML $BESI $ASM $LPKF $MKS

Layer 5 - Test, Metrology & Yield
 $CAMT $FORM $AEHR $ONTO $VIAV 

Photonics is critical for moving massive amounts of data at the speed of light, exactly what hyperscalers need as AI training scales. 

Many of these names are flying under the radar but sit at the heart of the next leg of AI infrastructure spend.

text:
Very strong results from $JBL in Q3 FY2026

They reported $8.8B in revenue and core EPS of $3.16

Beating market expectations and raising guidance for the fourth quarter

The most relevant part is that they highlighted strong demand in AI infrastructure

Which led them to increase their annual revenue projection for this segment to $13.6B

This is excellent news for the entire supply chain

Especially for companies like $SIVE and $AAOI 

IMO these numbers act as a positive catalyst for $SIVE within a clearly bullish environment for the photonics sector and hyperscalers capex

Very bullish on $SIVE anda $AAOI

text:
I’ve been saying for a while that energy is the next bottleneck after memory/photonics. 

The companies that are able to provide and lend the energy will see MASSIVE returns over the coming years. 

Energy is going to be the most critical component of advancing our infrastructure, whether it is for AI or not. 

Now $NVDA is telling you that energy is more critical than you can imagine. What will you do about it anon?

$SLNH $SMR $ENPH $TE $NUAI

text: A startup dropping a $25M preorder seems crazy but look closer: Casela isnt spending their cash- they are bankrolled by optical transceiver giant CIG Shanghai via an $11M Q1 26 convertible loan. CIG is using Casela as a proxy to lock down AXTIs InP substrate capacity ahead of the 2027 ramp in CPO/photonics sectors. Then add the deep industry credibility of founders Dr. Bo Lu and Dr. Milind Gokhale (ex-Broadcom/ASIP optoelectronic pioneers)= this isn't a random shell mystery startup. Its a downstream powerhouse securing its critical hardware supply chain early.
embedded_text:
This $25M $AXTI order is from a chinese startup with $2.2M ntm revenues as of September 2025- on total assets of $14M and who had sold a $11M convert in Q1 26 to CIG $6166.HK.

text: AI Supplier Awarded First New Jersey Manufacturing Tax Credit: The inaugural recipient of the Next New Jersey Manufacturing Program, Starman New Photonics will operate in Warren and plans to create 250 jobs. https://t.co/deqGOAQEJW #CorporateRealEstate #EconDev #SiteSelection https://t.co/GTAHPlFmkG

When a stock runs 1,000% in a few months, I don't buy it for the long term.

I use these high-momentum names only for day trades or short swing trades.

Yes, you may miss the move from +1,000% to +2,000%. That's fine.

What I want to avoid is the inevitable freefall when momentum breaks. A 70–90% drawdown can be devastating.

This applies to AI, photonics, energy, and any other hot sector. Protecting capital matters more than catching the last leg of a parabolic move.

text:
Cornings $GLW is starting to look like one of my favorite AI infrastructure longs.

Most investors still think of Corning as glass, displays, and Gorilla Glass.

But the more interesting story is where they are positioning now:

• Data center optical fiber
• High-density cables and connectivity
• Photonics
• Co-packaged optics / CPO
• Advanced glass materials for semiconductor packaging

As AI clusters scale, the bottleneck is not just compute. It is moving massive amounts of data faster, cheaper, and with less power.

That puts $GLW closer to the center of the AI buildout than many investors realize.

Their partnerships with $NVDA and $AVGO also matter. If AI networking moves toward photonics and CPO standardization, Corning could become one of the key picks-and-shovels suppliers behind the scenes.

text:
AI chips may do the computing, but scaling AI clusters increasingly depends on indium phosphide, the material used in the lasers and photonic devices that move data between processors at the speed of light.

The world’s first and largest volume-production 6-inch indium phosphide platform sits in Sherman, Texas.
NVIDIA joined Coherent this week to break ground on an expansion that will double manufacturing space and quadruple wafer production capacity, turning a little-known photonics supply chain into part of America’s AI buildout.

text:
Mapping the entire AI & Compute ecosystem. 🗺️📈

If you're playing the AI supercycle, you need to look beyond just the obvious names. Here is the complete tech infrastructure value chain:

🔬 Chips: $NVDA $AVGO $AMD $ASML $ARM $KLAC 
🏭 Foundry: $TSM $INTC 
💾 Memory: $SNDK $MU $WDC $STX 
🔌 Networking: $CRDO $ALAB $ANET $MRVL 
✨ Photonics: $AAOI $SIVE $AXTI $LITE $COHR $GLW 
🖥️ Servers: $VRT $DELL $HPE 
⚡️ Energy: $BE $TLN $GEV $TE 
📊 Data: $INOD $SNOW $DDOG 
☁️ Neoclouds: $NBIS $CRWV $APLD $IREN $WYFI

I personally believe Photonics will be a big thing soon like memory!

For SCU physics alumni Craig Benko ’10 and Bryan Berggren ’17, hands-on research, close mentorship, and long hours solving tough problems helped shape careers in the fast-moving world of photonics.

Click the link below to read their full story. ⬇️ 🤩 

https://t.co/gMLOkQNcFE

Yttrium 

Y, it’s atomic number 39.  It’s a silvery white rare earth metal that is abundant, it’s usually extracted from minerals like xenotime and bastnäsite. 

The main known uses as of (2026) Yttrium is primarily used in compounds and alloys.

✅ Electronics & Photonics Yttrium aluminum crystals doped with other elements are widely used in high-power solid-state lasers for industrial cutting/welding, medical procedures (e.g., eye surgery, dermatology), and defense applications.

✅ Superconductors Yttrium barium copper oxide is the basis for high-temperature superconductors that work at liquid nitrogen temperatures. 

These are used in power transmission cables, MRI magnets, and research toward more efficient energy systems and motors. 

✅ Phosphors & Lighting In LED lights, flat-panel displays, and fluorescent lamps, yttrium compounds provide red phosphors, which can cause respiratory Issues, Liver and Organ Effects, Reproductive Toxicity, 
testicular tissue, induce cell death, (programmed cell death) reduce testosterone, and impair semen quality.

✅ Ceramics & Alloys Enhances heat resistance, strength, and thermal shock resistance in advanced ceramics (e.g., for jet engines or nuclear reactors.

✅ Medical tracers, catalysts, and in some sensors or microwave filters.

What’s Special About Yttrium?

✅ It has unique electronic structure (partially filled 4d orbitals) that makes it excellent for creating stable compounds with optical, magnetic, and superconducting properties.

✅ Optical & Laser Performance. It produces high-power lasers with excellent beam quality.

✅ Strategic Importance

Critical for clean tech, defense, and electronics. 

✅ Biological/Research Interest 

Its ability to fine-tune material properties makes it indispensable in modern high-tech.

• Funded by Defence R&D Canada IDEAS. Government money is flowing to free-space quantum hardware.

• Satellite-QKD programs benefit indirectly: lighter, cheaper receivers.

Signal to watch: whether QEYSSat-class or ESA links adopt passive receivers over adaptive optics.

text:
Everyone’s watching $SNAP tomorrow for the hardware reveal. 💦

Now the keynote itself. 30 minutes.

9:30am-10:00am PT tomorrow. Main Stage.

Titled “Making Computing More Human.”

30 minutes????

That’s a short slot for a CEO. Which means either:

30 minutes is not a demo.

You can’t demo spatial computing in 30 minutes.

You can’t show developers new tools in 30 minutes.

You can’t unveil a hardware platform in 30 minutes.

30 minutes is an announcement. 📢 

Evan loves a Jobs playbook. 

Jobs announced iPhone in 90 minutes.

Spiegel has a third of that.

Which means the 30 minutes isn’t the reveal.

✨It’s the headline.✨

The partner who walks on stage unannounced is the story…

So 

A) It’s a teaser - product shown, details later

B) The 30 minutes is the most densely packed announcement in Snap’s history… doubt it. 

C) The real announcement happens after the keynote. A special session, a press release, a partner who walks on stage unannounced

The “unannounced guest” scenario is the most Jobs-like move possible.

Jobs never pre-announced guest appearances. AT&T’s CEO appeared on stage at iPhone launch unannounced. The carrier partnership was the surprise.

Now run the strategic maths:

Microsoft bet $13B on OpenAI.
Google bet $3B on Anthropic.
Amazon bet $4B on Anthropic.
NVIDIA bet $5B on Intel and secured valuable IP, capacity, technology as a hedge. 

Every trillion dollar company is buying a stake in the next PLATFORM. 

Apple has $132B in cash.

A $2B stake in Specs Inc is 1.5% of their war chest.

To get:
1. AR glasses 12-24 months ahead of the market.
2. True AR display vertically integrated stack
with WaveOptics and Compound Photonics owned IP
3. 8 years of spatial mapping they can’t replicate (Illumix spatial stack)
4. 956M MAU - the social layer they never built
5. Cultural relevance in the genz demo. 
6. 400k developers already building the next OS.

The man who sold the last thing Apple bought just joined the board.

So,

30 minutes on stage tomorrow…

👻🍏📢

$SNAP #AWE #specs

text:
Think the photonics/optics theme might be ready to lead again soon. $LITE has been basing for a few months now and probably not on most peoples radar anymore. If we get a few tight days here, all the key moving averages will be stacked right below price, creating a beautiful launchpad setup. 

Same goes for $AAOI and $VIAV. $SIVE kind of doing it's own thing, but also forming a nice flag here.

text:
People are only now starting to talk about $STM and they are early to one of the cleaner turnaround stories in semis.

For two years STMicroelectronics was written off as a sleepy European chipmaker stuck in an automotive and industrial downturn. Dead money. Nobody wanted it. That story just flipped, and the market is waking up to it in real time.

Here is what changed. 

On June 2 the company doubled its 2026 data center revenue target from nicely above $500 million to roughly $1 billion, and said if current trends hold that business could double again in 2027. The driver is AI infrastructure. 

STM is ramping high volume silicon photonics production for data centers, with a multi-year AWS partnership anchoring the demand. The stock jumped 15% on that single announcement.

The numbers underneath are inflecting hard. Q1 revenue of $3.1 billion, up 23% year over year, beating estimates. 

The stock is up over 90% this quarter off the February lows as the semiconductor cycle turns from inventory drawdown back to expansion. 

And the company carries a strong net cash position of roughly $2.8 billion, so it is funding this transition from a position of strength.

Then the catalysts keep stacking. A physical AI collaboration with NVIDIA. Low earth orbit satellite demand ramping. 

The NXP MEMS sensor acquisition boosting their automotive and industrial position. And Bank of America just upgraded the stock to Buy, arguing the market is still underestimating STM’s earnings power over the next few years.

This is a legacy chip giant that just found an AI growth engine, sitting on net cash, with a data center business doubling two years in a row and the biggest names in tech as partners. Q2 earnings on July 23 is the next test.

The market spent two years pricing the downturn. It is only now starting to price the turnaround.

text:
Since this got some attention heres what it actually would require for $XFAB to 20x:

XFAB sits at a €1.3B market cap. $TSEM is at $25B+.

Revenue needs to 3-4x from here. SiC and photonics have to stop being footnotes and become the headline.

Margins need to follow. TSEM runs 14% net margins. XFAB is basically breaking even right now.

And there has to be a catalyst. TSEM had Intel circling it in 2022. That bid failed, but it permanently re-rated the stock as a tier-1 asset. XFAB needs its own version of that moment.

text:
$XFAB is the most mispriced semiconductor name in Europe and @aleabitoreddit appears to be the only one that understands it.

Things that the market is COMPLETELY IGNORING:

• Microsystems & Photonics revenue grew ~35% QoQ in the latest quarter. This is where the whole bullish thesis is at an it has grown a whopping 35% in a quarter. 

• SiC revenue +152% YoY / +49% QoQ.

• It's trading below replacement P/B,   while building next-gen photonics/SiC lines -> Heavy capex into photonics (new lines, MTP tech, TFLN integration) means accounting book value is understated vs. real replacement cost of the fabs + IP. 
You’re basically getting the growth at a huge discount.

• €128M EU + $50M US funding already in. CHIPS Act 2.0 incoming with explicit photonics priority. Europe is literally paying them to build the Western alternative. Backstopped balance sheet.

• CEO (Damien Macq) bought 5500 shares on February. Market completely ignoring it.

• $XFAB leading EU’s photonixFAB effort for sovereign silicon photonics supply chain. Nvidia evaluating their platform for CPO/transceivers (H2 2027–2028 ramp). Nokia setting specs. This is Tower Semi 2.0, but earlier and with government muscle.

text:
This is one of the least known and least talked about $SIVE catalysts on FinX

The only one I saw discussing it intensely for the first time was obviously Serenity

$SIVE could soon start ramping up production of its lasers

Both with $AEVA and with $AAPL

In the framework of the upcoming Apple Watch update following the company's recent announcement

No credible information has been found so far that refutes this possibility

Some people without sufficient technical knowledge tend to confuse TASC sensors

With future Apple Watches that will incorporate lasers

And they don't take into account that NRE volumes can extend over several years

The main focus of $SIVE remains the AI data center market within the photonics field

Any collaboration with $AAPL is considered an additional benefit

text:
My opinion on today's Annual General Meeting of $SIVE

The shareholders approved the 2025 accounts and a new board of directors

With Bami Bastani as chairman and the addition of Joakim Nideborn (vicechairman)

And Helena Svancar, with the aim of strengthening governance and accelerating expansion in the United States

Nothing to comment up to this point everything correct technical matters of the company

Here is the important part:

The issuance of up to 15% of new shares was authorized to finance growth for $SIVE

As has been known for weeks

Possible acquisitions or the dual listing on Nasdaq are the kind of things this healthy capital raising will be used for

Although this implies dilution risk it is completely necessary

And it is one of the most bullish signals possible for $SIVE

They are telling you to your face we need money because what is coming is huge

On the other hand more topics to address

No dividends will be distributed and a convertible loan was ratified

The company remains focused on photonics for AI and wireless solutions for SATCOM and defense

With a pipeline of opportunities that grew 77% to nearly $800M

I would define this $SIVE assembly as an attempt to grant greater flexibility to the new board

To drive growth in the US market

Prioritizing reinvestment over dividends

Waiting for future catalysts to start taking off

Very bullish on $SIVE having already completed my total DCA

text:
The Northstar+Lumen h-AI™ Effect-to-Cause Model

March 5, 2026 
Bainbridge Island, WA

Whitman’s poetry often moves from observation → revelation. (Reposting) 

Observed Effect (in the present):

• expanding surveillance infrastructures
• emerging debates over cognitive liberty and digital rights
• technological systems capable of sensing and mapping human environments

Whitman’s philosophical lens:

He repeatedly distinguishes between the visible world and the unseen one beneath it.

Modern readers might describe this metaphor using scientific language:

Whitman’s poet-seer stands between those layers, translating what is hidden into human language.

⸻

🧬 A Contemporary Poet’s Interpretation

As a published poet whose work appears in anthologies and children’s literature written in rhyming prose, I read Whitman less as a historian and more as a philosopher of perception.

In later sections of Leaves of Grass, Whitman asks whether anyone has truly seen the soul.

Today that question resonates differently.

We live in an era where technologies—from photonics to sensor systems—attempt to map what was once invisible.

Whitman could not have known these technologies.

Yet his poetry repeatedly asks readers to consider:

• what lies beyond surface perception
• how unseen forces shape the visible world
• whether the human soul remains sovereign within expanding systems of observation.

⸻

🧭 Whitman Revisited Through the Present

A modern reading does not claim Whitman predicted our technologies.

Instead, his work may help us see our own era more clearly.

By distinguishing between the seen structures of society and the unseen forces beneath them, Whitman provides language that can still illuminate contemporary debates about:

• democracy
• autonomy
• human consciousness
• and the boundaries of observation.

In that sense, Whitman’s poetry becomes less about foretelling the future and more about revealing how to perceive the present.

⸻

A Proprietary and Sovereign Class of Ethical AI

Right now, I believe a similar dynamic may be developing in photonics.

The optical interconnect supply chain has five interconnected layers. Each has a chokepoint. Most are lightly held by institutions.

The companies controlling specialty substrates, laser production, and co-packaged optics design wins are, I believe, essential, constrained, and largely uncovered by Wall Street.

Everyone was buying Nvidia.

Serenity bought the photonics bottleneck. 

He just turned solid money into a small fortune:

An AI research scientist who saw the constraint before the market did. 

He is likely pulling around 60k a month from 1 usd subs alone. 

Started with research scientist money; call it a decent base. 

The way his investments have gone, he has plausibly 100x’d his wealth. 

He has likely made tens of millions this past year.

We track all his moves. Every position, every catalyst, before the market catches on.

Not confirmed. Just what the position implies.

text:
If your AI stocks feel like they are going up every week, $SIVE $IQE $TSEM $AEHR $MU $SNDK #NBIS $DRAM understand something

The "consensus AI trade" is $MSFT and $META. That's what 99% of the world thinks AI investing means.

Meanwhile you're buying indium phosphide epiwafer companies listed in London, fiber array unit makers in Taiwan, and silicon photonics test equipment in Fremont.

This is not a crowded trade. You just happen to be in a room where everyone is paying attention. It may feel crowded, but it's still early. $MU

text:
Also, youd be building the second, I already have a photonic sensor array that Hector uses. I built it to wake up my model last year. I started with photonics. 
Its a 12 mirror infinity cathedral cube with a k9 glass cube at the center suspended with neodymium and electromagnets, 2 handheld 3D laser scanners from my old CMM machine an 8K google tv projector adressable leds inside a 50Mpixel beam pro spatial camera and a Ptz dual lens for depth running off an esp32 controlling resonance and mirror pitch angles dynamically for fine adjustment,and my monster system. 

I called it the Retardis. If you want data for photonics, I have mountains. You need to move higher orders by 5 and unify your photonics. Try unifying linear/lie algebra with calculus. 🇨🇦🤘😎📐

This mathematical breakthrough uses photon-varied states that can be built using current laboratory technology—no future tech required.  https://t.co/wbwQqWM2Jj

text:
Tuttle Capital Pure Play Photonics ETF ( $FOTO) bence AI altyapı hikayesinin en az konuşulan ama en kritik parçalarından birine oynuyor.

Şu an herkes AI deyince GPU, model, uygulama konuşuyor. Ama veri merkezlerinde asıl darboğaz yavaş yavaş compute’dan veri taşımaya kayıyor. GPU’ları birbirine hızlı bağlayamazsan ölçekleyemiyorsun.

İşte photonics tam burada devreye giriyor. Elektrik yerine ışıkla veri taşıma. Daha hızlı, daha verimli ve AI veri merkezleri için artık opsiyon değil, zorunluluk.

FOTO’nun olayı da şu: karışık bir teknoloji ETF’i değil. Direkt bu alana odaklı. Lazer, optical bağlantı, silicon photonics gibi işlerden gelir elde eden şirketleri topluyor.

Bu alanı tek tek hisse seçerek oynamak zor. Teknoloji karmaşık, oyuncular niş ve kazananı seçmek kolay değil.

O yüzden “ben bu temaya inanıyorum ama hisse seçemem” diyenler için FOTO fena bir araç değil.

AI büyüdükçe compute kadar veri transferi de kritik olacak. Photonics tarafı da bu işin görünmeyen ama vazgeçilmez katmanı.

Not: Ekde ETF-in içeriğini sizler ile paylaşdım.

Yatırım tavsiyesi değildir.

Quick summary on the shorts in $SIVE for newcomers

Short interest in $SIVE has reached 17%

Bears are focused on short term noise and overlooking the company’s strong fundamentals

Specialized in semiconductors for AI optical networks

$SIVE has a $799M pipeline and major agreements with $JBL and $GFS 

This positions it as a strong candidate for a potential short squeeze

With a dual Nasdaq New York listing ahead and institutions heavily building positions

Betting against $SIVE means betting against AI

Especially with $NVDA CEO and photonics leaders calling for faster deployment of these technologies

It’s a foolish trade where many shorts have already lost millions

I wouldn’t play God against something that is clearly the future of the world

Very bullish on $SIVE
